generated_oml:
// C3: Physical sensing components
instance HokuyoUST10LXLidar : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Hokuyo UST-10LX LiDAR generating 10 Hz point clouds (32k points each)"
    DTDFVocab:producedData LiDARPointCloudDataTransmitted
]

instance IntelD435iDepthCamera : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Intel D435i depth camera providing 30 Hz depth frames (1920×1080 resolution)"
    DTDFVocab:producedData DepthCameraFrameDataTransmitted
]

instance WheelSpeedEncoder : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Wheel speed encoder sampling 100 Hz vehicle speed"
    DTDFVocab:producedData WheelSpeedDataTransmitted
]

instance SteeringPotentiometer : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Steering potentiometer providing 100 Hz steering angle"
    DTDFVocab:producedData SteeringAngleDataTransmitted
]

// C4: Data transmitted from sensors
instance LiDARPointCloudDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom HokuyoUST10LXLidar
]

instance DepthCameraFrameDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom IntelD435iDepthCamera
]

instance WheelSpeedDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom WheelSpeedEncoder
]

instance SteeringAngleDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom SteeringPotentiometer
]

// C10: Data instances
instance LiDARPointCloudData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Serialized LiDAR point cloud data for real‑time streaming"
    DTDFVocab:fromData LiDARPointCloudDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataTranslationFE
]

instance DepthCameraFrameData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Depth camera frame data for perception"
    DTDFVocab:fromData DepthCameraFrameDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataTranslationFE
]

instance WheelSpeedData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Vehicle wheel speed samples"
    DTDFVocab:fromData WheelSpeedDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataTranslationFE
]

instance SteeringAngleData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Vehicle steering angle samples"
    DTDFVocab:fromData SteeringAngleDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataTranslationFE
]

instance ObjectTrackStateData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Object track state vectors produced by DBSCAN + Kalman filter clustering of LiDAR point cloud"
    DTDFVocab:fromData LiDARPointCloudDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo AccControllerFE
]

instance DesiredSpeedData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Desired speed commands computed by ISO 15622 ACC controller"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo AccControllerFE
    DTDFVocab:inputTo AnalyticsFE
]

// C7: Models
instance PointCloudClusteringTrackingModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "DBSCAN clustering followed by Kalman filter tracking of LiDAR point clouds"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataTranslationFE
]

instance ACCControllerModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "ISO 15622 ACC controller computing desired speed"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo AccControllerFE
]

// C11: Enablers
instance DataTranslationFE : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Serializes and performs unit conversions before Wi‑Fi transmission"
    DTDFVocab:enables DataMonitoringService, AnalyticsService
]

instance AccControllerFE : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Executes ISO 15622 logic on the DT for real‑time speed command computation"
    DTDFVocab:enables ComputationOffloadingService
]

instance AnalyticsFE : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Stores trajectory and vehicle state data in SQL database for trend analysis"
    DTDFVocab:enables AnalyticsService
]

instance VisualizationFE : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Feeds 3D visualization FE with CAD rendering of vehicle and environment"
    DTDFVocab:enables VisualizationService
]

instance ReportingFE : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Generates reports from logged data via Reporting FE"
    DTDFVocab:enables DataMonitoringService
]

// C6: Services
instance DataMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Displays real‑time UI of speed, steering, object tracks, projected path; handles data logging"
    DTDFVocab:provides RealTimeSpeedAndTrackDisplayInsight,
                    HistoricalTrendAnalysisInsight
]

instance ComputationOffloadingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Offloads ACC controller computing desired speed from lead vehicle distance"
    DTDFVocab:provides LeadVehiclePredictionInsight,
                    ExcessiveFollowingDistanceAlertInsight,
                    ACCDiagnosticsInsight
]

instance RemoteManagementService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Handles operator commands to enable/disable ACC, modify speed, trigger emergency braking"
    DTDFVocab:provides EnableACCAction,
                    DisableACCAction,
                    ModifyDesiredSpeedAction,
                    EmergencyBrakeAction
]

instance VisualizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "3D CAD rendering of vehicle and surrounding environment"
    DTDFVocab:provides 3DVisualizationInsight
]

instance AnalyticsService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Logs trajectories and generates trend analytics"
    DTDFVocab:provides HistoricalTrendAnalysisInsight
]

// C17: Insights
instance RealTimeSpeedAndTrackDisplayInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Displays live vehicle speed, steering angle, and detected object tracks"
]

instance LeadVehiclePredictionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Predicts future acceleration/brake commands based on lead vehicle dynamics"
]

instance ExcessiveFollowingDistanceAlertInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Alerts when following distance exceeds safe threshold"
]

instance ACCDiagnosticsInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Diagnostics of ACC performance via reported speed error"
]

instance HistoricalTrendAnalysisInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Analyzes historical trajectory and speed trends from stored data"
]

instance 3DVisualizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Renders 3D view of vehicle and environment"
]

// C17: Actions
instance EnableACCAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Enables Adaptive Cruise Control"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance DisableACCAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Disables Adaptive Cruise Control"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance ModifyDesiredSpeedAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Adjusts desired speed setting of ACC controller"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance EmergencyBrakeAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Triggers emergency braking via brake actuator"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

// C1: Actuators
instance SteeringServoActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Electronic steering servo driven by steering command from DT"
]

instance MotorDriveActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Vehicle motor drive actuated by Vedder Electronic Speed Controller, commanded by DT"
]

instance BrakeActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Emergency brake actuated via hydraulic interface from DT command"
]

// C5: Environments
instance PhysicalTwinEntity : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains HokuyoUST10LXLidar,
                IntelD435iDepthCamera,
                WheelSpeedEncoder,
                SteeringPotentiometer,
                SteeringServoActuator,
                MotorDriveActuator,
                BrakeActuator
]

instance DigitalTwinEntity : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains LiDARPointCloudData,
                DepthCameraFrameData,
                WheelSpeedData,
                SteeringAngleData,
                ObjectTrackStateData,
                DesiredSpeedData,
                DataTranslationFE,
                AccControllerFE,
                AnalyticsFE,
                VisualizationFE,
                ReportingFE,
                DataMonitoringService,
                ComputationOffloadingService,
                RemoteManagementService,
                VisualizationService,
                AnalyticsService
]

instance UserWorkstationEntity : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains RemoteManagementService
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "DT outputs include desired speed commands transmitted to the ESC (via CAN over Wi‑Fi), emergency‑brake commands, and ACC enable/disable flags directed to the vehicle’s ECU. These messages are formatted by the Data Translation FE and sent as low‑latency packets (≤100 ms) to the PT’s on‑board controllers, which then actuate the motor and steering mechanisms."
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "All DT services are executed in real‑time with an end‑to‑end latency target of ≤100 ms from PT to DT; sensor data, control decisions, and actuator commands are synchronized to a global clock with an accuracy of ≤100 ms. The DT processes perception cycles at 10 Hz (LiDAR) and 30 Hz (depth camera) and updates control decisions at the same rates."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The DTS is orchestrated by four entities: (1) Physical Twin Entity hosting sensor‑data acquisition and low‑level actuation FE; (2) Digital Twin Entity (ACC controller, data‑collection, reporting, storage, visualization FEs); (3) User Entity (3D visualization FE, control‑panel FE); (4) Cross‑System Entity providing Data Translation FE. Communication flows over a Wi‑Fi network using DDS (UDP) between entities and ROS 2 topics within each entity."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "The DT lifecycle spans (1) Design/Requirements (R1–R8) establishing communication, data gathering, and control constraints; (2) Development/Implementation (mapping SW components to ISO 23247 FEs, coding ROS 2 packages, integrating third‑party tools); (3) Validation/Testing (latency verification, ACC correctness, safety‑critical compliance); (4) Deployment/Operation (continuous data collection during vehicle runs, remote management by operators); (5) Monitoring/Iteration (generating reports, refining ACC parameters)."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "Fidelity is ensured by employing high‑fidelity sensors (LiDAR, depth camera), low‑latency Wi‑Fi communication, and a validated ACC model per ISO 15622. Validation includes end‑to‑end latency measurement (≤100 ms), sensor‑to‑decision timing checks, and functional correctness testing of ACC logic under various traffic scenarios. Uncertainty is mitigated through sensor fusion (LiDAR + depth camera) and Kalman‑filter tracking. Safety compliance is addressed by adhering to ISO 15622 control logic and maintaining a human‑in‑the‑loop operator interface."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The DT communicates with the PT over an IEEE 802.11 (based on Wi‑Fi) link; data serialization is handled by the Data Translation FE. ROS 2 nodes exchange messages via DDS over UDP, enabling multicast of sensor data and control commands between entities. The end‑to‑end latency is measured and constrained to ≤100 ms."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "The PHYSICAL TWIN ENTITY runs on the vehicle’s integrated NVIDIA Jetson Orin Nano and ESC controllers; the DIGITAL TWIN ENTITY operates on the same edge node (NVIDIA Jetson Orin) or a nearby cloud placeholder, hosting ROS 2 master, data‑collection, ACC, and visualization services; the USER ENTITY is hosted on an operator workstation (Windows/Linux) via a web‑based UI served by the control‑panel FE."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The DT design follows ISO 23247 (Digital Twin Manufacturing Framework) for reference architecture, ISO 15622 for ACC safety logic, ROS 2 as middleware adhering to DDS/OPC UA interoperability, and optional OPC UA standard for data translation. Communication relies on open‑source Wi‑Fi (IEEE 802.11) and industry‑standard CAN‑bus interfaces."
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "The system is safety‑critical; end‑to‑end latency ≤100 ms and 100 ms clock sync ensure timely response. The ACC logic conforms to ISO 15622 safety guidelines. Human‑in‑the‑loop operator interface allows emergency brake overrides. No explicit cybersecurity measures (e.g., encryption, authentication) are detailed in the source documents."
]
